chunk_summary: Guidelines for evaluating childhood obesity in India, including comorbidity thresholds, referral criteria, and follow-up plans based on IAP and WHO standards, with specific numeric cutoffs for BMI, dysglycemia, and ALT levels.
learning_objectives: Understand the IAP and WHO thresholds for classifying obesity and comorbidities in Indian children.
Identify criteria for endocrine and genetic evaluations in pediatric obesity cases.
Interpret lab results for dysglycemia, MASLD/NAFLD risk, and hypertension in children.
Develop structured follow-up plans based on comorbidity-driven findings.
key_facts: IAP BMI-for-age cutoffs for Asian Indian children: ≥23 for overweight risk, ≥27 for obesity.
Prediabetes: fasting glucose 100–125 mg/dL or 2-hour OGTT 140–199 mg/dL; diabetes: fasting glucose ≥126 mg/dL or 2-hour OGTT ≥200 mg/dL.
ALT thresholds for MASLD/NAFLD risk: >26 U/L in males, >22 U/L in females; ALT >80 U/L requires expedited assessment.
Endocrine evaluation is recommended for obesity with short stature or falling height velocity.
Genetic evaluation is advised for obesity onset before 5 years, hyperphagia, consanguinity, dysmorphism, or developmental delay.
Follow-up intervals should be comorbidity-driven, with urgent referrals for diabetes-range glucose, ALT >80 U/L, or stage 2 hypertension.
reference_quote: Classify severity using the chart (under-5 WHO weight-for-height z-scores; 5–18 years IAP BMI-for-age with adult-equivalent cutoffs 23 for overweight risk and 27 for obesity in Asian Indian children).
